Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Settlement Agreement
Off Calendar Aug-03-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT BLUE & GOLD FLEET, L.P.'s Motion To Enforce Settlement Agreement.

Defendant Blue & Gold Fleet, L.P.'s motion to enforce settlement agreement is granted. In plaintiffs' counsel's declaration "acknowledging" the motion, counsel represents that his clients are aware of the motion and of the general release required by the settlement agreement, but that they have not executed the release or given him any instructions regarding the motion. Accordingly, the motion is unopposed, and will be granted.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-14-2018 Sustained
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Arnold Bershcler, Esq. 1-800-338-1441 of Berschler for Plaintiff Danmui Kao.
C. Joseph Ou, Esq. 415-348-6000 of Gibson Robb & Lindh for Defendant BLUE & GOLD FLEET, L.P.
Katelyn Knight, Dpuy City Attorney 707-648-4388 of the City Attorney's Office for Defendat City of Valeejo through court Call.

RULING - A R G U E D. Plaintiff argues maritime law, cites cases, duty of care. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentatove ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT BLUE & GOLD FLEET, L.P.'s DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT.

Defendant Blue & Gold Fleet, L.P.'s demurrer to the third cause of action for common carrier negligence in the complaint filed by plaintiffs Danmui Kao and Raymond McClellan is sustained without leave to amend. As alleged in the complaint, maritime law applies in this case, which provides that a duty of reasonable care is owed to non-crew members aboard on a ship. California law imposing a heightened duty of care on common carriers is inapplicable because it directly conflicts with maritime law. Plaintiffs' reliance on case law prior to Kermarec v. Compagnie Generale Transatlantique (1959) 358 US 625 is misplaced since Kermarec established and/or confirmed a nationwide rule that supersedes all case law to the contrary.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported. (D302/HK)
